text: Players that aren’t scared to fail are often the most successful. Chet has played scared this series. Shrunk from the moment the first 4 games. It’s hurt OKC. Jered McCain? He DGAF. He’s shootin’. While shooting too much can be a problem, he and Caruso were the first to challenge Wemby at the rim. To go right at him. McCain had the highlight of game 3 by scoring over Wemby and flexing. OKC absolutely needed him to not care and just let it fly. I actually believe Isaiah Joe is the better overall player, especially considering defense, but he won’t shoot the ball unless he’s open. He won’t force the shot. It’s why McCain passed him in the rotation.
label: analysis

text: Situation and Opportunity Can Make a Star. Brunson, Jokic, Ginóbili, Arenas, Draymond, etc etc. Second round picks to make multiple All Star games, some are HOFers, with Jokic being all-time, and there are more. I sometimes wonder who else is out there that, had they been given the opportunity, would have been able to piece together an elite career. I named 2nd round picks, but also someone like Jimmy Butler, who I believe was 30, and Tony Parker
label: analysis

label: analysis

text: De'Aaron Fox is the Julius Randle of PGs: a player who can raise your floor but not your ceiling, can put up strong numbers but doesn’t really elevate teammates and not a great hustle player or defender. label: analysis

text: Chet needs to take the Wemby challenge upon himself from here on. Challenge Wemby on both ends of the floor. Chet is not nearly the player or potential of Wemby but he’s playing small, timid and, at times, frightened. Take the ball to the rim. Make Wemby block your shot. Make Wemby dunk over you. Don’t play soft. Chet is talented for his size but shrinks in this matchup and disappears from far too many games.

text: Alex has the league's highest defensive rating among players who have played more than half of the season's games, and also the only one with a point allowed percentage below 100 points per 100 possessions – 99.3. Furthermore, his closest pursuer in adequate minutes, his Oklahoma City teammate Chet Holmgren, is a full three points behind Alex – he has 102.3 points allowed per 100 possessions. Pat Connathan is also in between, but he played only 7 garbage minutes per game for the Hornets, and his defensive rating is closer to Holmgren than to Caruso.
label: analysis

text: I want to compare the top 10 NBA scorers based on Jordan’s 1072 games. My contention is that MJ is the best because he did so much while playing relatively few games, so I’d like to see how Kobe, James, etc stack up if they had played the same number of regular season games. For reference most of the top scorers are also near the top for most games played while Jordan is tied for 113.
label: analysis

text: OKC/LA- OKC in 5 - No Luka early on so I have to assume OKC wins the first 2 games. It’s imperative LA wins the next two at home regardless of who is available if they want any semblance of a chance. If Luka is still not available OKC sweeps. This series is actually more interesting than people make it out to be with the confidence LA role players have had (and any LeBron James led team is an interesting watch) but OKC is the worst possible matchup for LA.
label: analysis

text: NYK/PHI- NYK in 6 - New York seemed to be in dire straights against ATL going down 2-0 but they then rang off 4 straight wins. Their last 3 playoff wins have been 29, 51, and now 39 point win margins. They are playing their best basketball at the perfect time.
label: analysis

text: DET/ CLE- CLE in 6 - Detroit barely made it out of the first round thanks to Cade Cunningham playing his best ball of the season against Orlando. text: A big guard or wing like Luka could grab 10 rebounds and it's not seen as detrimental because of the increase in pushing the pace and getting a shot off before the defense sets. label: analysis

text: The rise of the 3pt shot and it’s naturally longer and more sporadic rebounds have made rebounding less of a skill and more luck. We also see teams having the guards do more rebounding on the easy ones simply bc it’s faster than having the big get it and pass to the guard. We also have more missed shots than ever bc of the faster pace and increased number of 3s.
